completed_slices.summary: Implemented daemon-owned completion publication: close records and report artifacts are committed before final_sha is advertised, close failures become structured incidents, and resume after publication is idempotent.
public_interfaces_changed: Runtime handoff/final-report final_sha now points to the post-publication integration branch HEAD.
slice close incidents now include structured slice_id, path, message, and policy payload fields.

acceptance_status.criterion: A successful run advertises one final handoff SHA/branch state that contains both worker implementation changes and daemon-owned publication changes such as closed slice JSON and final report artifacts; close/report truth is not stranded on an unadvertised integration-branch commit.
acceptance_status.evidence: run_slices now writes close records and both reports, commits them via commit_completion_publication, then sets runtime summary.final_sha to integration HEAD. daemon_fake_run_handoff_and_inspect_black_box asserts handoff final_sha equals branch HEAD and that the SHA contains closed slice JSON plus final-report artifact.

acceptance_status.criterion: A regression test covers the R8 failure shape: after gate success and slice closure, handoff/final report evidence points at a commit that includes the close records instead of only the implementation commit.
acceptance_status.evidence: daemon_fake_run_handoff_and_inspect_black_box now reads runtime final-report.json, checks final_report.final_sha equals handoff.final_sha, and git-show verifies that SHA contains status=closed and closed_by_run.

acceptance_status.criterion: If slice metadata needed for closure is absent or unwritable, Khazad-Doom records a structured incident naming the affected slice/path and preserves or blocks handoff readiness according to explicit policy; it must not silently drop close-record truth.
acceptance_status.evidence: artifact::SliceClosureIncident records severity, kind, slice_id, path, message, and policy. Missing metadata emits slice_close_skipped with preserve_handoff_ready_missing_metadata; close read/write/validation errors emit slice_close_failed with block_handoff_on_close_failure and manager returns blocked. Untracked metadata black-box test asserts structured event fields.

acceptance_status.criterion: Successful-run publication is resume-safe and idempotent: resuming after publication does not duplicate close/report commits, lose final SHA truth, or re-close already published slices incorrectly.
acceptance_status.evidence: completion_publication_is_current skips close/report publication when reports already exist and slice metadata is already closed by the run; close_slice_file no-ops for same-run closure. resume_after_completion_publication_is_idempotent simulates interruption after publication and verifies unchanged branch HEAD, one publication commit, unchanged close JSON, and preserved final_sha.

acceptance_status.criterion: Handoff and final-report exit states remain read-only summaries over daemon lifecycle state; this slice must not introduce remote push/PR mutation or a second source of workflow truth.
acceptance_status.evidence: No remote push/PR paths or run/slice statuses were changed. exit_states are still derived from gate/completed slice state; handoff still only executes remote actions under existing explicit push/create-pr flags.

acceptance_status.criterion: Existing fake-runner end-to-end, handoff, incident, and resume tests remain green under the full verification profile.
acceptance_status.evidence: Ran khazad-doom slices validate plus the three required black-box cargo tests; all passed. Added and ran resume_after_completion_publication_is_idempotent; it passed.

assumptions: Committed .workflow/reports files are included in the advertised publication commit; runtime final-report.json and handoff JSON carry the final commit SHA after that commit is created.

integration_repair.summary: integration gate passed; integration_repair=auto skipped repair
